title: OpenClaw memory status should preserve config context when resolving fallback providers

source.license_note: Localized ACBench reproduction derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62855.

task.summary: Repair memory status resolution so fallback provider lookup keeps the effective config context.
task.instructions: Investigate why status --deep loses config context during fallback provider resolution, repair the config-threading path, and preserve the existing rendering behavior when counts are present.
task.acceptance_notes: Alternative fixes are allowed as long as fallback provider resolution sees the right config.

visible_context.issue_text: A healthy memory provider can be reported as invalid because fallback embedding resolution is called without the current config context.
error_logs: memory-lancedb-pro invalid config: embedding: must have required property 'embedding'
the same plugin still reports vector ready and fts ready
reproduction_steps: Run the unit tests from the repository root.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models the config-resolution path behind the false invalid-config message.

code_fault.description: Fallback provider resolution drops the current config context during status evaluation.

constraints.notes: This GitHub-derived reproduction is expected to be repaired in code, not by changing tests.

notes: Code-only localized reproduction of openclaw/openclaw issue #62855.
